segregr 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

    Calculate segregation metrics

    measure_segregation.Rd

    Calculate segregation metrics

    measure_segregation(
  data,
  id_field = "id",
  distance_method = "geodist",
  weight_function = "gaussian",
  bandwidths = 0
)

    Arguments

    data

    a spatial sf object of the study area divided into areal units (such as census tracts), containing the columns 'id', 'geometry', and one column per population group indicating that group's population count in each areal unit.

    id_field

    character. Name of the identifier column in data. Defaults to "id".

    distance_method

    character. Method used to compute distances between areal unit centroids. Either "geodist" (default, uses the 'geodist' package for fast geodesic distances) or "sf" (uses sf::st_distance()).

    weight_function

    character. Spatial kernel function applied to distances. Either "gaussian" (default) or "step" (binary kernel that weights all units within the bandwidth equally).

    bandwidths

    numeric vector. One or more bandwidths in metres controlling the spatial scale of analysis. Use 0 to compute aspatial (classical) metrics. Multiple values produce results for each bandwidth. Defaults to 0.

    Value

    A named list (class segreg) containing:

    areal_units

    sf object with the input geometries.

    groups

    character vector of population group names.

    bandwidth

    numeric vector of bandwidths used.

    D

    data.frame of global Dissimilarity index per bandwidth.

    E

    scalar global Entropy.

    H

    data.frame of global Information Theory index H per bandwidth.

    d

    data.frame of local dissimilarity values.

    h

    data.frame of local entropy and H values.

    P

    data.frame of global pairwise Exposure indices.

    Q

    data.frame of global Isolation indices per group.

    p

    data.frame of local pairwise Exposure indices.

    q

    data.frame of local Isolation indices.

    Examples

    
library(sf)
library(segregr)

# load sample data from package segregr
marilia_sf <- st_read(system.file("extdata/marilia_2010.gpkg", package = "segregr"))
#> Reading layer `marilia_2010_proj' from data source 
#>   `/private/var/folders/g1/9h7mr4ss5390lzy0s_x82_440000gn/T/Rtmpaqyzax/temp_libpath787948ab8ec0/segregr/extdata/marilia_2010.gpkg' 
#>   using driver `GPKG'
#> Simple feature collection with 295 features and 10 fields
#> Geometry type: POLYGON
#> Dimension:     XY
#> Bounding box:  xmin: 597725.2 ymin: 7535553 xmax: 612714.8 ymax: 7551388
#> Projected CRS: SIRGAS 2000 / UTM zone 22S

# calculate segregation metrics
segregation <- measure_segregation(marilia_sf)

# global dissimilarity index
segregation$D
#>       bw         D
#>    <num>     <num>
#> 1:     0 0.2749453

# global entropy
segregation$E
#> [1] 1.733732

# global information theory index H
segregation$H
#>       bw        H
#>    <num>    <num>
#> 1:     0 0.110396
